tests.title: Community Garden Project
tests.subtitle: Organisers describe how this gardening project developed over time.
tests.text: Community Garden Project started several years ago after a group of teenagers wanted to spend more time learning about gardening. At first, meetings were small and took place in a local community centre every Saturday morning. [11]The organisers originally expected only a few students to join the project[/11]. However, interest grew quickly after photos and videos appeared online. Soon, students from neighbouring schools also wanted to participate. Some activities were difficult in the beginning because equipment was limited and organisers had little experience planning large events. Even so, members enjoyed working together and learning from mistakes. After a few months, older students began helping newcomers during practical sessions. [12]Organisers discovered that beginners learned faster when experienced students explained tasks simply[/12]. The project later received support from local businesses, which provided additional materials and meeting spaces. Parents were pleased because participants became more confident and responsible outside school. Several former members eventually chose university courses connected with gardening. [13]The organisers were surprised that the project influenced future study decisions[/13]. Today, meetings include discussions, practical activities and opportunities for students to present their ideas. Many participants say the social side of the project is just as important as learning new skills. Recently, organisers have created online tutorials so members can continue practising at home. [14]The organisers believe steady practice is more important than immediate success[/14]. Although the programme is larger now, organisers still try to keep the atmosphere relaxed and welcoming for everyone. Students often encourage nervous newcomers to join activities and ask questions freely. [15]The organisers hope participants become more confident about trying unfamiliar experiences[/15].

questions.question: What did the organisers expect at the beginning?

options.A: Only a small number of students would join.
options.B: The project would become internationally famous.
options.D: Students from many schools would attend immediately.

questions.explanation: The organisers thought only a few students would participate.

questions.question: What helped beginners improve more quickly?
Why were the organisers surprised later?

options.A: The project affected students’ future studies.

questions.explanation: Organisers did not expect study decisions to change.

questions.question: What do the organisers believe is important?
What is one main aim of the project?

options.A: Helping students feel confident trying new things.

questions.explanation: The project encourages confidence with new experiences.
